questions.statement: Which force is required to keep a particle in uniform circular motion?
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: If an object moves with constant acceleration then which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: <span style="">Its velocity increases linearly with time</span> /
Its acceleration will change with time /
For uniform circular motion, there will be the constant centripetal acceleration. /
More than one of the above /

questions.statement: An object completes 120 revolutions in 30 seconds along a circular path. The ratio of its frequency to its period is:&nbsp;
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: <div>A particle is moving in a uniform circular motion with constant speed V along a circle of radius r. The acceleration of the particle is</div>

A projectile fired with initial velocity u at some angle&nbsp;<span style="">&theta;</span> has a range R. If the initial velocity be doubled keeping the angle of projection same, then the range will be
A ball is dropped from moving bus. The ball will hit the ground following ____________
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: A bus increases its speed from 36km/h to 72km/h in 10 seconds. Its acceleration is.
options: 1m/s<span style="position: relative;  line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em;font-size:10.5px;">2</span> /
2m/s<span style="position: relative;  line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em;font-size:10.5px;">2</span> /
3.6m/s<span style="position: relative;  line-height: 0; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em;font-size:10.5px;">2</span> /
More than one of the above&nbsp; &nbsp; /

questions.statement: Which of the following statement is<strong> incorrect </strong>about a body undergoing a uniform circular motion?
options: In Uniform Circular Motion the body moving in circle is accelerating /
In Uniform Circular Motion the body is changing direction but the speed remains constant /
In Uniform Circular Motion the velocity of body remains constant /
More than one of the above&nbsp;<br /> /

questions.statement: If a body A of mass &lsquo;m&rsquo; is thrown with velocity &lsquo;u&rsquo; at angle 30&deg; to the horizontal and another body B of the same mass is thrown with the same velocity at an angle of 60&deg; to the horizontal, the ratio of horizontal range of A to B will be:
options: More than one of the above&nbsp;<br /> /

questions.statement: The velocity of a particle moving along the x - axis varies as a function of time t as v (t) = (1 - 3t<sup>2</sup>&nbsp;+ 2t<sup>3</sup>) m/s. If its position at t = 0 is x = 0 then at t = 2 s, its position is&nbsp;
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement:
<p>An object travels from point A to point B as shown in the figure. The distance and the magnitude of displacement for the motion are respectively</p>

options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: If a body falling freely under gravity from a height h, what will be the velocity as it hits the ground
options: More than one of the above<br /> /

questions.statement: A&nbsp;boat&nbsp;traveling perpendicular to the flow of&nbsp;the river&nbsp;is found at a distance of 50 km from the starting point after 2 hours of starting. If the&nbsp;boats travel with the same&nbsp;speed&nbsp;in upstream direction then the&nbsp;speed&nbsp;is 17 Km/hr, what is the&nbsp;speed&nbsp;of the&nbsp;boat&nbsp;and&nbsp;river&nbsp;(km/hr)?
options: More than one of the above /
More than one of the above /

questions.statement: <span style="">A particle is projected horizontally with a speed of 5 m/s from the top of a plane. The plane is inclined at an angle of 45&deg; with the horizontal. The particle strikes the plane at a distance d (measured from the point of projection) along the plane. The value of d is close to (take g = 10 m/s</span><span style=" line-height: 0; position: relative; vertical-align: baseline; top: -0.5em; font-size:10.5px;">2</span><span style="">)</span>
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: Which of the following position-time graphs represent non-uniform motion, where the rate of change in position is decreasing?
options: More than one of the above. /

questions.statement: A person moves 30 m north and then 20 m towards east and finally <span class="math-tex">\(30 \sqrt{2}\)</span>&nbsp;m in south west direction. The displacement of the person from the origin will be

A ball is thrown at 30&deg;&nbsp;with horizontal from the top of roof 20 m high with a speed of 13 m.s<sup>-1</sup>. At what distance from the throwing point will the ball, once again be at a height of 20 m from the ground? (g = 10 m.s<sup>-2</sup>)
A projectile has range on a horizontal plane equal to three times the maximum height attained by it. If u be the velocity of projection of the projectile, then its time of flight is:

<p>A car moves on a horizontal road of radius 16 m with increasing speed at a constant rate of 3 m/s<sup>2</sup>. If the coefficient of friction between the road and tyre is 0.5, then the minimum speed at which car will skid is&nbsp;</p>

<p>(assume g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>)</p>

A projectile can have the same range R for two angles of projection. If t<sub>1</sub> and t<sub>2</sub> can be the times of flight in the two cases then what is the product of the two times of flight?
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: For a body projected at an angle with the horizontal from the ground, choose the correct statement.
options: Gravitational potential energy is maximum at the highest point. /
The horizontal component of velocity is zero at highest point. /
The vertical component of momentum is maximum at the highest point. /
More than one of the above /

questions.statement: <p dir="ltr" style="line-height:1.38;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt;"><span id="docs-internal-guid-edf6b8b6-7fff-2b60-6e03-88d644630c83"><span style="  background-color: transparent; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; vertical-align: baseline; white-space-collapse: preserve;">A car is moving on a horizontal curved road with radius 50 m . The approximate maximum speed of car will be, if friction between tyres and road is 0.34 . [Take <span class="math-tex">\(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\)</span> ]</span></span></p>
options: More than one of the above /

questions.statement: A projectile is projected with velocity u and angle&nbsp;&theta; with the horizontal. What will be maximum height attained by the projectile?
options: More than one of the above /
